strengths: 19 years of backend and distributed-systems expertise, including low-latency, high-throughput infrastructure handling millions of requests per second
Currently contributing to agentic AI infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq, showing active trajectory toward core role responsibilities
Demonstrated systems-thinking and architectural judgment across multiple domains with strong open-source leadership and educational engagement
concerns: No demonstrated engineering leadership track record: resume lacks evidence of managing teams, hiring, or scaling from 10 to 30+ people as required for a CTO role
Multi-agent AI systems production experience is very recent (1 month at re:cinq) and unsubstantiated; no track record of designing, building, or operating agentic workflows in production
No fintech infrastructure background in credit systems, payment flows, or regulatory compliance; RIDE Capital is fintech-adjacent but lacks substantive detail

key_factors.factor: Engineering team leadership and scaling experience

response.justification: Szabo brings strong technical depth in backend systems and distributed infrastructure over 19 years, with emerging expertise in agentic AI frameworks. However, he lacks two core CTO requirements: no demonstrated engineering leadership or team-scaling experience (critical for an executive role), and only one month of unsubstantiated multi-agent AI production work. His current Staff Engineer role is too junior and recent to establish executive credentials. He is better suited for a VP Engineering or Principal Architect role focused on AI infrastructure than for a CTO position.
